claim: Double 'II' framework (Information + Implementation) separates agent instructions/memory/errors from Python scripts, enabling agents to self-improve iteratively
actionable: Structure your agent repos: Information folder holds instructions/memory/plans, Implementation folder holds scripts; agents update Information when they fail
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Agents Work 24/7 For Free

claim: Mac Mini with agent triggers and local execution (no API keys needed) is ideal for computer-use agents and workflows requiring platform access
actionable: For agents needing UI automation or social platform access (Instagram, Shopify), run them on Mac Mini locally with dummy HDMI; avoids IP-flagging issues
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Agents Work 24/7 For Free

claim: Modal serverless cloud lets agents run on cron schedules for free/cheap (hasn't exceeded $0.01 across multiple agents) with up to 60min execution per run
actionable: Use Modal for scheduled agents (hourly, daily, weekly tasks); free tier likely covers most use cases; don't use Modal for computer-use agents
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Agents Work 24/7 For Free

claim: Modal volumes store Information/Implementation folders; agents running on Modal can read/update these files before executing scripts
actionable: In Modal deployments, use volumes to store agent memory; agents load knowledge before executing, enabling persistent context across runs
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Agents Work 24/7 For Free

claim: Claude Code agents can self-improve: when they hit errors, they update the Information file and re-run, recursively perfecting workflows
actionable: Build agents to catch their own errors and update their instruction/memory files instead of failing; use recursive retry patterns
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Agents Work 24/7 For Free
